 {"id":"beads_rust-9u2","title":"EPIC: Interactive TUI Mode with Ratatui","description":"# Interactive TUI Mode with Ratatui

## Background & Rationale

Based on research of mature TUI tools (beads_viewer with Bubbletea, cass with Ratatui) and 2025-2026 terminal UI best practices, br would benefit from an optional interactive TUI mode for users who prefer visual exploration over command-line invocations.

### Why This Matters
- beads_viewer (bv) provides TUI for Go beads but requires separate installation
- Integrated TUI mode gives users choice without extra tools
- TUI enables rapid exploration of issue graphs and dependencies
- Visual feedback improves UX for complex operations
- Follows the dual-mode pattern: TUI for humans, CLI for agents

## Goals
Deliver an optional TUI mode using Ratatui that allows users to browse, filter, and manage issues interactively while maintaining the CLI-first design philosophy.

## In-Scope (v1 TUI)
- `br tui` or `br -i` to launch interactive mode
- Issue list view with filtering and sorting
- Issue detail view with full content
- Dependency graph visualization (ASCII art)
- Keyboard navigation (vim-style bindings)
- Search/filter as you type
- Quick actions (close, update status, add comment)
- Theme support (light/dark, custom colors)

## Out-of-Scope (v1)
- Full feature parity with bv
- Graph metrics (PageRank, betweenness) - use bv for that
- Real-time collaboration features
- Mouse support (keyboard-first)

## Technical Approach

### Ratatui Stack
```toml
[dependencies]
ratatui = "0.30"
crossterm = "0.28"
tui-textarea = "0.7"     # Text input
tui-tree-widget = "0.22" # Tree views
```

### Architecture (Elm-inspired)
```rust
struct App {
    state: AppState,
    issues: Vec<Issue>,
    selected: Option<usize>,
    filter: String,
    mode: Mode,
}

enum Mode {
    List,
    Detail,
    Search,
    Action,
}

enum Message {
    KeyPress(KeyEvent),
    IssueSelected(String),
    FilterChanged(String),
    ActionCompleted(Result<(), Error>),
}

fn update(app: &mut App, msg: Message) -> Option<Command> { ... }
fn view(app: &App) -> impl Widget { ... }
```

### Styling with Ratatui
```rust
use ratatui::style::{Color, Modifier, Style};

let priority_style = match issue.priority {
    0 => Style::default().fg(Color::Red).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
    1 => Style::default().fg(Color::Yellow),
    2 => Style::default().fg(Color::White),
    _ => Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray),
};
```

### Feature Flag
```toml
[features]
default = []
tui = ["ratatui", "crossterm", "tui-textarea"]
```

Build with TUI: `cargo build --features tui`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `br tui` launches interactive mode
- Issue list displays with priority colors
- Keyboard navigation works smoothly
- Filter/search is responsive
- Quick actions update database correctly
- Graceful exit restores terminal state
- Works on Linux, macOS, Windows Terminal

## Views

### List View
```
┌─ br - 24 issues ready ─────────────────────────────┐
│ Filter: [                    ] [↑↓] Navigate [q] Quit │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● P0 beads_rust-8f8  EPIC: Port beads to Rust      │
│ ● P1 beads_rust-0v1  Sync safety hardening         │
│ ○ P1 beads_rust-1ce  Phase 3: Relations & Search   │
│ ○ P2 beads_rust-4z6  Colored Terminal Output       │
│   ...                                              │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Enter] View  [c] Close  [u] Update  [/] Search    │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Detail View
```
┌─ beads_rust-8f8 ───────────────────────────────────┐
│ EPIC: Port beads (SQLite+JSONL) to Rust as 'br'   │
│ Priority: P0 (Critical)  Status: OPEN  Type: epic │
│ Owner: jeff141421@gmail.com                        │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ## Description                                     │
│ Deliver a classic, non-invasive Rust port of bd   │
│ with full JSONL/SQLite parity and command...      │
│                                                    │
│ ## Children (5)                                    │
│   ✓ Phase 1: Foundation                           │
│   ✓ Phase 2: Core Commands                        │
│   ○ Phase 3: Relations & Search                   │
│   ...                                             │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Esc] Back  [c] Close  [e] Edit  [d] Add dep      │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## References
- Ratatui: https://ratatui.rs/
- Ratatui Templates: https://github.com/ratatui/templates
- awesome-ratatui: https://github.com/ratatui/awesome-ratatui
- beads_viewer Bubbletea architecture
- cass TUI implementation

 {"id":"beads_rust-9yw1","title":"Extend format module with rich output support","description":"# Task: Extend Format Module with Rich Output Support

## CRITICAL: Existing Architecture
The codebase ALREADY HAS a format module at `src/format/`:
- `src/format/mod.rs` - Module exports
- `src/format/text.rs` - Text formatting with `colored` crate
- `src/format/output.rs` - Serializable output types
- `src/format/csv.rs` - CSV export

**DO NOT create a new src/output/ directory!**

## What to Do
EXTEND the existing `src/format/` module by adding:

### 1. File: `src/format/rich.rs`
Rich output components using rich_rust.

```rust
//! Rich terminal output components using rich_rust.
//!
//! This module provides enhanced visual output for TTY terminals
//! while preserving backward compatibility with existing format functions.

use rich_rust::{Table, Panel, Tree, Console, Style};
use crate::model::{Issue, IssueType, Status, Priority};

// Components defined here, integrated with existing color patterns
```

### 2. File: `src/format/context.rs`
OutputContext for mode detection.

```rust
//! Output context and mode detection.
//!
//! Determines whether to use Rich, Plain, JSON, or Quiet output
//! based on CLI flags and terminal detection.

use std::io::IsTerminal;

pub enum OutputMode {
    Rich,   // TTY with colors and rich formatting
    Plain,  // TTY but --no-color specified
    Json,   // --json flag
    Quiet,  // --quiet flag
}

pub struct OutputContext {
    mode: OutputMode,
    width: Option<usize>,
    // Integration with existing CliOverrides
}
```

### 3. File: `src/format/theme.rs`
Theme definitions that EXTEND existing color patterns.

```rust
//! Theme and color definitions for rich output.
//!
//! These EXTEND the existing color patterns in text.rs:
//! - Status: green (open) → yellow (in_progress) → red (blocked) → gray (closed)
//! - Priority: red+bold (P0) → red → yellow → gray
//!
//! Do NOT duplicate - import from text.rs where possible.
```

### 4. Update: `src/format/mod.rs`
Add new modules to exports:

```rust
pub mod text;     // existing
pub mod output;   // existing
pub mod csv;      // existing
pub mod rich;     // NEW
pub mod context;  // NEW
pub mod theme;    // NEW

pub use context::{OutputContext, OutputMode};
pub use theme::Theme;
```

## Why This Structure
- Preserves existing format functions unchanged
- Adds rich output as an enhancement layer
- Reuses existing color definitions
- Single module for all formatting concerns

## Existing Patterns to Preserve
From `src/format/text.rs`:
- `format_status()` - Status badges with icons
- `format_priority()` - Priority badges
- `format_type()` - Type badges
- `terminal_width()` - Terminal width detection
- `truncate_title()` - Smart title truncation

Dependencies:
  -> beads_rust-q0ws (blocks) - Add rich_rust dependency to Cargo.toml

Dependents:
  <- beads_rust-nh5h (blocks) - Implement Theme with semantic colors and styles
  <- beads_rust-38mz (blocks) - Implement OutputContext with mode detection","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:26:03.235238527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:39:59.361502594Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:39:59.361452389Z","close_reason":"Implemented all required components: rich.rs (RichIssueTable, RichIssuePanel, RichDependencyTree), context.rs (OutputContext, OutputMode), theme.rs (Theme with semantic styles). All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-9yw1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-q0ws","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-a02o","title":"create command ignores preopened storage context","description":"Fresh-eyes audit of beads_rust-84lm traced br create through src/main.rs and src/cli/commands/create.rs. main.rs preopens storage whenever a mutating command needs auto-flush, but the Commands::Create arm still calls commands::create::execute(&args, ...) instead of create::execute_with_storage(..., Some(open_result)). create.rs already exposes execute_with_storage specifically to reuse a pre-opened connection and avoid a redundant second open that can compete with WAL/write-lock behavior. Current dispatch defeats that path, reopens config/storage after startup/auto-import, and then phase-5 auto-flush runs on the separate preopened context rather than the command context. Expected: route Create through execute_with_storage when storage_result is available, or otherwise remove the misleading preopen path for create.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:50:12.844278040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:25:43.111756449Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-a02o","title":"create command ignores preopened storage context","description":"Fresh-eyes audit of beads_rust-84lm traced br create through src/main.rs and src/cli/commands/create.rs. main.rs preopens storage whenever a mutating command needs auto-flush, but the Commands::Create arm still calls commands::create::execute(&args, ...) instead of create::execute_with_storage(..., Some(open_result)). create.rs already exposes execute_with_storage specifically to reuse a pre-opened connection and avoid a redundant second open that can compete with WAL/write-lock behavior. Current dispatch defeats that path, reopens config/storage after startup/auto-import, and then phase-5 auto-flush runs on the separate preopened context rather than the command context. Expected: route Create through execute_with_storage when storage_result is available, or otherwise remove the misleading preopen path for create.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:50:12.844278040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:36:28.836963993Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:36:28.836378768Z","close_reason":"Create now consumes the preopened storage context and auto-flushes on the same context, with regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-a1y","title":"doctor Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br doctor` command for diagnosing and fixing common issues with the beads database and configuration.

## CLI Interface
```
br doctor [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --fix                       Attempt to fix detected issues
  --json                      Output as JSON
  --robot                     Machine-readable output
```

## Health Checks

### 1. Database Integrity
```rust
fn check_db_integrity(&self) -> Result<HealthCheck> {
    // SQLite integrity check
    let result: String = self.conn.query_row(
        "PRAGMA integrity_check",
        [],
        |row| row.get(0),
    )?;
    
    if result == "ok" {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Pass("Database integrity"))
    } else {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Fail("Database corruption detected", result))
    }
}
```

### 2. Schema Version
```rust
fn check_schema_version(&self) -> Result<HealthCheck> {
    let db_version = self.get_schema_version()?;
    if db_version == SCHEMA_VERSION {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Pass("Schema version"))
    } else if db_version < SCHEMA_VERSION {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Warn("Schema outdated", "Run br migrate"))
    } else {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Fail("Schema too new", "Update br"))
    }
}
```

### 3. Orphaned Dependencies
```rust
fn check_orphaned_deps(&self) -> Result<HealthCheck> {
    let orphans: Vec<String> = self.conn.prepare(
        "SELECT d.issue_id FROM dependencies d
         LEFT JOIN issues i ON d.depends_on_id = i.id
         WHERE i.id IS NULL"
    )?.query_map([], |row| row.get(0))?
      .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?;
    
    if orphans.is_empty() {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Pass("No orphaned dependencies"))
    } else {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Warn(
            &format!("{} orphaned dependencies", orphans.len()),
            "Run br doctor --fix to remove",
        ))
    }
}
```

### 4. Blocked Cache Consistency
```rust
fn check_blocked_cache(&self) -> Result<HealthCheck> {
    // Recompute blocked issues and compare to cache
    let computed = self.compute_blocked_issues()?;
    let cached = self.get_cached_blocked_issues()?;
    
    if computed == cached {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Pass("Blocked cache consistent"))
    } else {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Warn(
            "Blocked cache stale",
            "Run br doctor --fix to rebuild",
        ))
    }
}
```

### 5. JSONL Sync Status
```rust
fn check_sync_status(&self) -> Result<HealthCheck> {
    let db_mtime = self.get_db_mtime()?;
    let jsonl_mtime = self.get_jsonl_mtime()?;
    
    match (db_mtime, jsonl_mtime) {
        (Some(d), Some(j)) if d > j => {
            Ok(HealthCheck::Warn("JSONL out of sync", "Run br sync"))
        }
        (Some(d), Some(j)) if j > d => {
            Ok(HealthCheck::Warn("Database out of sync", "Run br sync"))
        }
        _ => Ok(HealthCheck::Pass("Sync status OK")),
    }
}
```

### 6. Dependency Cycles
```rust
fn check_cycles(&self) -> Result<HealthCheck> {
    let cycles = self.detect_cycles()?;
    if cycles.is_empty() {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Pass("No dependency cycles"))
    } else {
        Ok(HealthCheck::Warn(
            &format!("{} dependency cycles", cycles.len()),
            "Review with br dep cycles",
        ))
    }
}
```

## Output Format

```
br doctor

Checking beads health...

✓ Database integrity            OK
✓ Schema version               v3 (current)
⚠ Orphaned dependencies        2 found
  → Run br doctor --fix to remove
✓ Blocked cache                Consistent
⚠ JSONL sync                   Database is newer
  → Run br sync --flush-only
✓ Dependency cycles            None

Summary: 4 passed, 2 warnings, 0 errors

Run: br doctor --fix to address warnings
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Check database integrity
- [ ] Check schema version
- [ ] Detect orphaned dependencies
- [ ] Validate blocked_issues cache
- [ ] Check sync status
- [ ] Detect dependency cycles
- [ ] Fix mode to address issues
- [ ] Summary with pass/warn/fail counts

## Dependencies
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer
- Requires dep Command (cycle detection)
- Requires sync infrastructure

## Rationale
Doctor provides peace of mind that the database is healthy. It's especially useful after git merges that might introduce inconsistencies, or when issues seem to behave unexpectedly.

 {"id":"beads_rust-a3ui","title":"Define HASH_DISPLAY_TRUNCATION_LEN const for sync display","description":"SUBTASK 3.4: The number 12 is used twice in src/cli/commands/sync.rs for truncating content hashes in display output:

Line 443: if hash.len() > 12 { format!("{}...", &hash[..12]) }
Line 751: if result.content_hash.len() > 12 { format!("{}...", &result.content_hash[..12]) }

Both truncate SHA-256 hashes to 12 characters for human-readable display (full SHA-256 hex is 64 chars).

CHANGES:
- Add in cli/commands/sync.rs (or a shared location): const HASH_DISPLAY_TRUNCATION_LEN: usize = 12;
- Replace both > 12 and [..12] with > HASH_DISPLAY_TRUNCATION_LEN and [..HASH_DISPLAY_TRUNCATION_LEN]

WHY 12: 12 hex chars = 48 bits of entropy, which is more than enough to visually distinguish hashes in a project with <100K issues. Git uses 7-12 chars for abbreviated commit hashes for similar reasons.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:04.093019126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:37.576308154Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:37.575839304Z","close_reason":"Added HASH_DISPLAY_TRUNCATION_LEN const in cli/commands/sync.rs, replaced both truncation sites","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
